title: Connect the Anydocs MCP Server in Codex
description: Give Codex real access to Anydocs domain tools.

render.markdown:
# Connect the Anydocs MCP Server in Codex

A practical setup is to register `@anydocs/mcp` as a `stdio` MCP server and keep a minimal `AGENTS.md` file in the project root.

That tells Codex to:

1. call `project_open` first
2. inspect `project_open.authoring` for templates, resources, and resource templates
3. read `anydocs://authoring/guidance` when it needs the detailed workflow
4. inspect a template's `metadataSchema` before writing `template` / `metadata`
5. read with `page_list`, `page_find`, and `page_get`
6. write with `page_create_from_template`, `page_create`, `page_update`, `page_set_status`, and navigation tools
render.plainText: Connect the Anydocs MCP Server in Codex by registering @anydocs/mcp as a stdio MCP server and keeping a minimal AGENTS.md file in the project root. Codex should start with project_open, inspect authoring resources, read anydocs://authoring/guidance when needed, and check template metadataSchema before writing template metadata.
